intro:
Counseling can be powerful for navigating and overcoming life's obstacles, but it's important to understand that it is a process that takes time. While some people may experience relief after just a few sessions, meaningful and lasting change is often a slow and gradual journey.

Read the steps below to get started with Morning Light Counseling.

steps.title: Scheduling request
steps.description: Complete the initial scheduling request inquiry by filling out the form on the contact page. Someone will respond within 2 business days.

steps.title: Intake paperwork
steps.description: You will be given a link to the client portal where you will complete initial intake paperwork and be able to schedule appointments.

steps.title: A brief phone call
steps.description: Time will be set for a brief phone call to discuss any questions you might have prior to the first session.

closing:
We live in a fallen world, and we experience its brokenness every day. This brokenness impacts our lives deeply; we feel it both within ourselves as well as in the circumstances coming towards us.

Counseling provides a way to make sense of these experiences. Through telling our story, we can begin to understand our journey. Vitally, your personal story isn't isolated; it's intricately woven into the greater story of God's redemptive plan for fallen humanity.

faqs.question: What insurances do you accept?
faqs.answer:
Highmark, Blue Cross/Blue Shield, Capital Blue Cross, Geisinger, United Health Care (UHC | UBH), Optum, Aetna, and Quest and Quest Behavioral Health EAP.

Out of network providers: I'll gladly submit claims to your participating insurance provider on your behalf. Please remember that co-pays, deductibles, and any amounts not covered by your insurance remain your responsibility.

Because of federal regulations governing Medicare and Medicaid, I am unable to accept clients with these insurances, even for self-paid services. I am not in-network with these programs.

faqs.question: What is your self-pay rate?
faqs.answer: Self-pay rate is $125 per session. If you're paying without insurance, you're entitled to a Good Faith Estimate of costs before we begin — see the Good Faith Estimate page for details.

faqs.question: What ages do you see in office?
faqs.answer: Generally, I see ages 12 and older. Please reach out if you have any specific questions related to this. On occasion I will see younger ages depending on maturity level and willingness to engage in the counseling process.

faqs.question: Can we meet online?
faqs.answer: Yes. I see men in person at my Manheim office and by secure video anywhere in Pennsylvania using an embedded, HIPPA compliant platform. If meeting online, please ensure that you have both a proper connection and a private meeting space.

faqs.question: Is what I say confidential?
faqs.answer: Yes. What you share in counseling is protected by law and professional ethics. The narrow exceptions — like an imminent risk of serious harm to yourself or someone else, or suspected abuse of a child — are ones I'll walk you through plainly before we start.
